untranslatable_reason: Signal requires historical frequency analysis of past price movements (90% of time stock moved 28% in 10 months). This requires backtesting historical returns distribution and calculating empirical probability of reaching a move target—a meta-analysis not expressible as a single-bar indicator condition. The core requirement is: 'if historical data shows stock reached X% move in Y timeframe 90% of the time, then buy straddle.' This needs custom historical probability calculation logic beyond point-in-time indicators.
translation_notes: Attempted proxy: hvol_20 > 0.28 approximates high volatility regime where large moves are probable, but does not capture the specific historical frequency threshold (90%) or the precise 28% move distance over 10-month lookback period that defines this signal. A proper implementation requires custom historical backtesting logic to compute: frequency(abs(price_change_10m) >= 0.28 * entry_price) and filter entry only when this frequency >= 0.90.
